question.text: In tax-efficient retirement income planning, which strategy helps minimize equity LTCG tax?

answer.text: Redeem all equity investments in a single financial year
Spread equity redemptions across financial years to utilize the Rs.1,25,000 annual LTCG exemption each year
Avoid equity investments entirely in retirement
Hold equity forever without any redemption

explanation.text: **The Logic:** By spreading equity redemptions across financial years, retirees can utilize the Rs.1,25,000 annual LTCG exemption each year, minimizing total tax outgo. Redeeming everything at once wastes this recurring benefit. **The Trap:** Lump-sum redemption in one year means only one exemption of Rs.1,25,000 is available, while the rest is taxed at 12.5%. Spreading across years multiplies the tax benefit. **Cross-Exam Context:** Post Budget 2025, equity LTCG is taxed at 12.5% with Rs.1,25,000 annual exemption. Tax planning in the distribution phase can significantly improve net returns. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Model the tax savings for clients. For example, spreading Rs.10 lakhs of gains across 4 years saves approximately Rs.47,000 in tax compared to redeeming all at once.
